teamObjective.explanation: Partner (player 2) holds three cards and opponents are short; as the opening leader with a very weak hand, the priority is to play the lowest possible single so partner can most easily beat it and take control.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner likely wants to help team 0 finish. With three cards they could hold a mix of singles and possibly a pair; they have had no chance to signal beyond their card count. They are more likely to be able to beat a 3 than any higher lead.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponent player 1 is down to two cards (opponent_near_finish) and is likely trying to exit quickly, so they will cover a low lead if they can. Opponent player 3 holds three cards and will also try to seize control to stop partner. No bombs or high cards have been revealed yet.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the 3 of clubs is the lowest non-trump single available. It maximizes the chance that partner can cover it with a higher single, transferring the lead, while preserving my slightly higher clubs for future tricks.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: The 6 is my highest card and the hardest for partner to beat; if partner only has low singles, this wastes the chance to hand them the lead.
Still relatively high and gives partner a smaller window to beat it compared with the 3.
Better than 5/6 but still harder for partner to cover than the 3, with no compensating benefit.

risks: Partner may be unable to beat a single lead if their cards are all lower, are a pair, or are trumps they wish to conserve.
The near-finish opponent (player 1) may have a higher single or trump and win the trick, gaining control and possibly exiting.
Even if opponents take this trick, my remaining 4/5/6 are weak and may not regain control.
riskAssessment.mitigation: Keeping 4/5/6 preserves my highest cards for future leads. The low lead minimizes my own commitment and gives partner the best chance to take over.

notes: Hand is uniformly weak with no trumps, bombs, or combinations. Cooperative-optimal play is to facilitate partner by leading the lowest card.
